dependencies: Limited number of domestic suppliers of locomotives
Limited number of steel producers for rail
High barriers to entry for locomotive equipment suppliers
risks: Supplier discontinuation of locomotive manufacturing or parts
Inability to manufacture locomotives meeting efficiency or regulatory emissions standards
Consolidation or mergers in steel rail industry
Significant cost increases for rail purchases
Difficulty obtaining sufficient rail for maintenance and projects
Increased tariffs on imported goods affecting rail purchases

regulatory_risks: Surface Transportation Board (STB) jurisdiction over rates, service, and operations
STB proposed rulemaking on reciprocal switching and commodity exemptions
STB new procedures for smaller rate complaints under appellate court review
STB exploration of changes to railroad revenue adequacy methodology
STB authority to regulate service including emergency service orders
Federal Railroad Administration (FRA) regulations
Department of Transportation (DOT) regulations
Occupational Safety and Health Administration regulations
Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration regulations
Department of Homeland Security (DHS) regulations
Transportation Security Administration (TSA) regulations including cybersecurity requirements
State and local environmental agency regulations
Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) regulations
California Air Resources Board (CARB) regulations
Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) regulations
Resource Conservation and Recovery Act compliance
Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act (Superfund) compliance
Clean Air Act compliance
Clean Water Act compliance
Railway Labor Act (RLA) collective bargaining requirements
Common carrier obligations requiring transport of hazardous materials
Positive Train Control (PTC) requirements
Hazardous materials routing rules
Rail Security Sensitive Materials (RSSM) tracking and documentation requirements
Changes to federal corporate tax rates
Enactment of new tax laws or tariffs
Revision in tax regulations
United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA) compliance
International trade agreement changes
Potential expansion of railroad operations and pricing regulation
Climate change related restrictions, caps, taxes, or controls on greenhouse gas emissions
Government mandates on premature adoption of unproven technology

sanction_risks: International armed conflicts including Russia-Ukraine war
International armed conflicts including Israel-Hamas war
Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East
Deterioration of security for international trade
Changes to U.S. trade policy affecting international shipping demand
Shifts in patterns of international trade
Actions of taxing authorities affecting customers doing business in foreign countries
Significant interruptions of trade with Mexico, Canada, or Southeast Asia countries including China
